 {"id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","title":"A5.2: tag hot cells with microarchitectural signatures","description":"## Goal
tag hot cells with microarchitectural signatures

## Checklist
1. Tag each hot cell with the dominant signature such as HITM, remote NUMA, LLC or TLB pressure, branch waste, or durability pressure.
2. Mark mixed or ambiguous cases honestly.
3. Keep the tags usable by architecture and tuning work.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Tag each hot cell with the dominant signature such as HITM, remote NUMA, LLC or TLB pressure, branch waste, or durability pressure.
2. Mark mixed or ambiguous cases honestly.
3. Keep the tags usable by architecture and tuning work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Each hot cell is tagged with one or more dominant microarchitectural signatures such as HITM/ownership ping-pong, remote NUMA traffic, LLC pressure, TLB pressure, branch waste, front-end starvation, or durability pressure, with mixed cases marked honestly.
2. The tags are tied back to canonical matrix rows, hardware signature, placement profile, and the specific counters or traces that justified them.
3. The output is directly usable by architecture, tuning, and prioritization work without reopening raw perf-counter dumps.
4. Ambiguous or mixed cases remain explicit instead of being forced into a false single-label story.","notes":"Classification obligations:
- translate raw topology-aware counter and trace data into a compact microarchitectural signature map for each hot cell
- require provenance back to the specific row, hardware profile, placement profile, and capture source that justified the tag
- preserve mixed/ambiguous classifications so later architecture work can see when a row is multi-bound instead of pretending one culprit explains everything
- optimize for users by turning counter archaeology into a reusable steering artifact rather than a one-off expert memory","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:02.629775278Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:05:27.871730230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","statistics","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2355,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting A5.2 immediately after closing A5.1. -{"id":"bd-db300.1.6","title":"A6: capture hardware topology, affinity, NUMA/SMT state, and placement policy","description":"## Goal
Capture the hardware-topology and placement contract for this optimization campaign so results can be interpreted correctly on high-end Threadripper and Xeon-class machines. This includes socket, node, CCD/CCX/LLC topology where discoverable, SMT sibling relationships, preferred-core and boost behavior, memory topology, pinning policy, memory-placement policy, and any runtime environment details that materially affect write-path scaling.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Artifact bundles record CPU model, stepping, and microcode or firmware revision where accessible; socket or NUMA topology; CCD/CCX/LLC domains where discoverable; SMT state; memory topology or NUMA distances; kernel version; governor or EPP/energy-policy state; boost/C-state state; huge-page or THP state; relevant scheduler or IRQ placement settings; and the benchmark placement profile.
2. The benchmark methodology states when pinning, SMT spreading or packing, NUMA-local allocation, interleave, automatic NUMA balancing, IRQ pinning, compile/helper-lane placement, or other placement strategies are used or intentionally avoided.
3. Future many-core measurements can be reproduced on comparable hardware without rediscovering the environment contract.
4. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 2. The benchmark methodology states when pinning, SMT spreading or packing, NUMA-local allocation, interleave, automatic NUMA balancing, IRQ pinning, compile/helper-lane placement, or other placement strategies are used or intentionally avoided.
3. Future many-core measurements can be reproduced on comparable hardware without rediscovering the environment contract.
4.  {"id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","title":"A6.1: capture topology firmware and power-state discovery bundle","description":"## Goal
capture topology firmware and power-state discovery bundle

## Checklist
1. Capture CPU model, firmware or microcode where possible, socket or node layout, SMT relationships, boost or EPP state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings.
2. Tie the bundle to benchmark sessions.
3. Keep the capture format reusable by manifests and reports.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Capture CPU model, firmware or microcode where possible, socket or node layout, SMT relationships, boost or EPP state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings.
2. Tie the bundle to benchmark sessions.
3. Keep the capture format reusable by manifests and reports.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The discovery bundle records the canonical hardware signature fields needed for run identity, including CPU model/stepping, microcode or firmware where available, socket/NUMA/LLC topology, SMT relationships, boost or governor state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings.
2. The bundle is tied mechanically to benchmark sessions and artifact manifests using the stable naming/provenance contract, so future runs do not invent ad hoc hardware IDs or paths.
3. The result is directly reusable by A3.3 manifests, G6.1 run-identity logging, and operator-facing reports without translation.
4. The bead makes it obvious when hardware identity is incomplete or partially unavailable instead of silently pretending the environment is fully characterized.","notes":"Discovery-bundle obligations:
- define the canonical hardware signature fields that become part of run identity, including CPU model/stepping, microcode or firmware where available, socket or NUMA layout, LLC domains, SMT relationships, boost/governor state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings
- bind the discovery bundle to the stable artifact naming/provenance contract so hardware identity is mechanically attached to runs rather than stored as an informal side note
- make partial discovery explicit, including which fields are unavailable, inferred, or intentionally omitted
- optimize for users by making environment drift and machine-to-machine mismatch visible before benchmark results are interpreted too confidently","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackHill","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:29.954161942Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:34:15.989945215Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T06:34:03.240401770Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","measurement","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:25:10.693021565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2364,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","author":"BlackHill","text":"Claiming this bead on a low-conflict surface.  {"id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","title":"A6.2: define execution contract for baseline pinned and adversarial placement profiles","description":"## Goal
define execution contract for baseline pinned and adversarial placement profiles

## Checklist
1. Define exact execution rules for baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial profiles.
2. Spell out pinning, SMT policy, memory policy, and helper-lane placement.
3. Make the contract ready for G7 and Track H reuse.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Define exact execution rules for baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial profiles.
2. Spell out pinning, SMT policy, memory policy, and helper-lane placement.
3. Make the contract ready for G7 and Track H reuse.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The execution contract defines baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial placement profiles with exact rules for CPU pinning, SMT packing or spreading, NUMA or memory policy, helper-lane placement, and any required environment disclosures.
2. Each profile includes a user-facing invocation contract showing how suites and focused reruns select it, which knobs are fixed versus optional, and when the profile is mandatory for a claim.
3. The contract is stable enough that G7 suites, Track H comparison work, and future operators can reproduce placement-sensitive runs without hidden tribal knowledge.
4. The result makes it clear when a run violated the placement contract and therefore cannot be compared cleanly.","notes":"Execution-contract obligations:
- define baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial placement profiles concretely enough that humans and agents can invoke them without guesswork
- specify CPU pinning, SMT policy, memory placement, helper-lane placement, and required environment disclosures for each profile
- align profile naming and run identity with the stable artifact naming/provenance contract so operators can predict artifact locations and compare like with like
- include operator guidance on when each profile is required, optional, or intentionally avoided
- optimize for users by preventing false comparisons caused by silent drift in pinning or memory policy between runs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:44.131929556Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T07:47:40.237157184Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T07:26:25.061417210Z","close_reason":"Completed: exact baseline/recommended/adversarial placement execution contract added to the live campaign manifest/types/docs with targeted verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","db300-program","db300-track-a","parallelism","perf","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:25:22.293473399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2372,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed after closing bd-db300.1.4.2 via fresh bv robot triage.  {"id":"bd-db300.1.6.3","title":"A6.3: capture IRQ helper-lane and memory-policy side conditions","description":"## Goal
capture IRQ helper-lane and memory-policy side conditions

## Checklist
1. Record IRQ placement, helper-lane placement, NUMA allocation policy, interleave or membind behavior, and automatic NUMA balancing state.
2. Distinguish intentional from accidental settings.
3. Make the data consumable by tuning and verification work.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-db300.7.5.3","title":"G5.3: define evidence ownership rerun entrypoints and gap-conversion rules","description":"## Goal
Define evidence ownership, rerun entrypoints, and gap-conversion rules so verification is operational instead of aspirational.

## Checklist
1. Record the expected evidence bundle, rerun entrypoint, and owner for each verification item.
2. Define when an uncovered verification requirement must become a new bead.
3. Keep the output usable by operators and CI without reconstructing the plan by hand.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every verification item names its evidence bundle, rerun entrypoint, and owner.
- The rule for converting gaps into tracked work is explicit.
- The result is directly usable by CI and operators.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Each verification item names its expected evidence bundle, rerun entrypoint, and owner. 2. The bead defines when a missing verification obligation must become a new bead. 3. The matrix is usable by CI and operators without reconstructing context.","notes":"This bead makes verification operational rather than aspirational.

Operational requirement:
- each verification obligation must name an owner, the expected artifact directory or manifest key, the rerun command/script entrypoint, and the pass/fail signature that CI and humans should look for
- define explicit gap-conversion rules: when a required test/e2e/log artifact is missing, future work must either create a new bead or record an accepted temporary gap with reason
- keep the output compact enough for operators but specific enough that future agents do not have to reverse-engineer how to rerun evidence for a claimed win or regression","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:00:37.356010235Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:39:28.081289475Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","db300-program","db300-track-g","evidence","quality-gate","track-g","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.7.6","title":"G6: add structured logging contracts for unit, e2e, and perf validation","description":"## Goal
Define the structured logging contract for unit, e2e, and performance validation so many-core regressions are diagnosable without ad hoc instrumentation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The schema covers run identity, topology or placement, primitive versions, failure context, and rerun metadata.
- Unit, e2e, perf, and failure paths emit the required fields where relevant.
- Artifact manifests and failure bundles are mechanically comparable and operator-friendly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The logging schema covers fixture, workload, mode, run identifier, source revision, Beads data hash, hardware profile, topology or placement scenario, affinity or memory policy, primitive or policy version, retries or conflicts, queue depth, wake-to-run or parking stats where relevant, seeds, key timings or counters, failure context, and an explicit rerun command or script reference.
2. Unit-test helpers, e2e scripts, or benchmark harnesses touched by this epic emit logs rich enough to reproduce and debug failures, including topology-sensitive failures.
3. Failure bundles and artifact manifests are consistent enough to compare runs mechanically and useful enough to attach directly to CI or operator reports.
